1: Two-panel before/after diagram. LEFT: a robot agent reading a neat organized context window (label 'GOOD CONTEXT'), arrow to a green check (label 'CORRECT'). RIGHT: the same robot reading a faded, crossed-out, cluttered context (label 'DECAYED CONTEXT'), arrow to a red cross (label 'CONFIDENT ERROR').
2: A left-to-right row of five small boxes each labeled '95%', joined by multiplication signs, leading by an arrow to a final highlighted box labeled '~77%'. A small bracket below labeled 'errors compound'.
3: A tall document/context column. The top band (label 'START') and bottom band (label 'END') are crisp and highlighted in the accent color. The large middle band is faded grey (label 'MIDDLE: LOST'). A small eye icon on the side with arrows pointing only to the top and bottom edges.
4: A horizontal progress bar filled to about 60 percent in the accent color with a victory flag planted at the 60 percent mark (label 'DONE'), while the remaining 40 percent is greyed and dashed (label 'ACTUALLY LEFT').
5: A bell curve. The tall central mass is labeled 'AVERAGE CASE'. The thin far-right tail is labeled 'WORST CASE'. A vertical accent line marks the worst-case tail with a small flag labeled 'DESIGN HERE'.
6: Two stacked paths. TOP path: a robot rushing straight into a tangle of many tool actions ending in a red cross (label 'ACT FIRST'). BOTTOM path: a robot drawing a small plan then taking one clean action ending in a green check (label 'THINK FIRST').
7: One question on the left fans out into three parallel reasoning paths (labels 'PATH 1', 'PATH 2', 'PATH 3'), each producing an answer, all feeding a tally box labeled 'VOTE' that outputs the majority answer.
8: A decision tree. At the very first node it forks into several lightly-drawn explored branches (label 'EXPLORE'), then one bold accent branch is chosen (label 'COMMIT'). A small note near the root labeled 'irreversible'.
9: A balance scale. Left pan holds a small intricate gear cluster (label 'CLEVER SCAFFOLDING'). Right pan holds a big simple block plus a stack of chips (label 'COMPUTE'). The right pan tips down as the winner.
10: An accuracy curve over thinking-steps. It rises to a peak (small flag labeled 'BEST ANSWER') then clearly declines afterward (label 'OVERTHINKING'). A vertical marker at the peak labeled 'STOP HERE'.
11: A vertical quality bar trying to rise but stopped by a hard horizontal ceiling line (label 'RETRIEVAL'). The capped bar is labeled 'ANSWER'. A small note: 'answer can't exceed retrieval'.
12: A funnel labeled 'RETRIEVAL'. A big cloud of error marks enters the top; far fewer come out the bottom, but a few error marks still slip through (label 'STILL POSSIBLE'). Not zero.
13: A model in the center fed by two trays. Tray one: a few clean items (label 'RELEVANT') leading to a green check. Tray two: many near-identical look-alike items (label 'NEAR-MISSES') leading to a confused red cross.
14: A simple bar comparison of two retrieval scores: a bar labeled 'SEMANTIC' and a slightly taller bar labeled 'KEYWORD'. A third tallest bar labeled 'HYBRID' marked as best with a small star.
15: On the left, three stacked tiers of a pyramid labeled top to bottom 'WORKING', 'SHORT-TERM', 'LONG-TERM' (label 'MEMORY SYSTEM'). On the right, a single oversized empty box labeled 'BIGGER PROMPT' with a red cross over it.
16: Two toolboxes. Left: a cluttered box overflowing with many dull tools feeding a confused robot with a red cross (label '30 DULL'). Right: three clean sharp tools feeding a confident robot with a green check (label '3 SHARP').
17: A horizontal pipeline. Rigid square brackets at the input and output ends (labels 'CODE' at both edges) bound a soft fluid blob in the middle (label 'MODEL'). A note: 'deterministic edges'.
18: A gate. A path toward a box labeled 'AUTONOMY' is blocked by a closed gate that only unlocks when a panel of gauges and trace lines (label 'OBSERVABILITY') is present. Without traces the gate shows a lock.
19: One large wobbly box with a red cross (label 'UNRELIABLE') transformed by an arrow into several small tidy boxes each with a green check (label 'SMALL & RELIABLE'). Not a single bigger box.
20: A vertical ladder of fixes from cheapest at the bottom to most expensive at the top: rungs labeled bottom-up 'PROMPT', 'TOOLS', 'FINE-TUNE', 'NEW PLATFORM'. A hand reaches for the lowest rung first (small label 'start here').
21: Two tools handed to a robot. The first has a clear attached label tag (label 'CLEAR DESCRIPTION') and is used correctly with a green check. The second is unlabeled (label '???') and is misused with a red cross.
22: A dense block of paragraph lines with a red cross over it (label 'PROSE'), an arrow pointing to a clean simple worked example block with a green check (label 'EXAMPLE').
23: Two side-by-side meters. Left meter pinned to full (label 'CONFIDENCE 100%'). Right meter at about two thirds (label 'ACCURACY 70%'). The gap between them bracketed and labeled 'MISCALIBRATED'.
24: An ambiguous input splitting into two possible meanings. A robot raises a flag with a question mark choosing to ask (label 'ASK', green check), while a path that confidently picks one meaning is crossed out (label 'GUESS', red cross).
25: A single tall bar labeled '97% OVERALL' that looks healthy. Below it, a broken-down row of segment bars reveals one short segment labeled '60%'. A note: 'averages hide failures'.
26: Left: an eye glancing over a few outputs, fine (label 'VIBES'). Right: the same eye overwhelmed by a huge wall of outputs, breaking down (label 'AT SCALE'), with an unused ruler nearby labeled 'EVAL'.
27: A magnifying glass examining raw rows of data (label 'LOOK AT DATA') with a green check, while a crowd of figures walks past ignoring it (label 'SKIPPED FIRST'). A small tag: 'highest ROI'.
28: A balance scale acting as an LLM judge. A physically long answer block on one pan outweighs a short correct answer on the other (label 'LENGTH WINS'). A note: 'biased by length'.
29: A gauge with its needle pushed hard to maximum (label 'METRIC'), while behind it the real structure it was meant to measure is crumbling (label 'REAL GOAL'). A note: 'metric becomes the goal'.
30: A repaired crack protected by a small shield labeled 'TEST' stays sealed with a green check. Beside it, an identical crack with no shield silently reopens via a loop arrow (label 'REGRESSION', red cross).
31: A clean three-circle Venn diagram. Circles labeled 'PRIVATE DATA', 'UNTRUSTED CONTENT', and 'EXFILTRATION'. The center overlap is filled with the accent color, holds a small skull, and is labeled 'DANGER'. A small tag: 'pick at most 2'.
32: A horizontal stream of identical token shapes. Two are pointed out as indistinguishable: one labeled 'YOUR INSTRUCTION', one labeled 'ATTACKER'. A robot looks at them unable to tell apart (label 'NO BADGES').
33: A robot agent holding a ring of keys labeled 'YOUR PRIVILEGES'. A shadowy hand on the side points the robot toward a forbidden door (label 'ATTACKER DIRECTS'). The robot complies. A small tag: 'confused deputy'.
34: A privileged 'PLANNER' box on one side, separated by a solid barrier wall from a pool of 'UNTRUSTED DATA' on the other. The planner issues orchestration arrows but the barrier blocks it from reading the poison.
35: A robot agent enclosed inside a thick-walled box labeled 'SANDBOX'. A small contained burst is trapped inside the walls (label 'CONTAINED'). Limited reach arrows stop at the wall.
36: A fork choice. Left option: a simple straight arrow pipeline (label 'WORKFLOW', green check, tag 'cheap, predictable'). Right option: a tangled looping agent (label 'AGENT', tag 'flexible, costly'). The simple one is selected.
37: A cascade. A query enters a small 'CHEAP MODEL' which resolves most cases (label 'MOST'); only the few hard cases escalate up an arrow to a big 'EXPENSIVE MODEL' (label 'HARD ONLY').
38: A row of agents multiplying from one to many, each agent dropping a coin onto a steeply rising stack of money (label 'TOKEN COST'). A note: 'multi-agent tax'.
39: On top, an org chart of three team boxes. Below, a system architecture of three matching module boxes connected the exact same way, with dashed lines mapping team to module (label 'CONWAY').
40: An action box receiving a retry arrow that loops it twice, but a stamp labeled 'IDEMPOTENCY KEY' ensures only one effect (green check). Beside it, the same double retry with no key causes a double effect (label 'CHARGED TWICE', red cross).
41: A circuit-breaker switch on a line between a caller and a sparking, failing service. The switch is flipped to OPEN, cutting the line so no more calls go through (label 'STOP CALLING').
42: A large bank of machines smoothly handling the bulk of work (label 'EASY 99%'), while one tiny lone human is left straining over a single hard fragment (label 'HARD 1%'). A note: 'the leftover job is harder'.
43: A person with eyes shut following a glowing dial/screen, while the real contradicting evidence sits ignored right in front of them with an arrow (label 'TRUSTS MACHINE'). Tag: 'automation bias'.
44: An autonomy slider with four notches on the left aligned against a vertical 'STAKES' meter on the right. Low stakes line up with high autonomy; high stakes line up with low autonomy (label 'MATCH LEVEL TO STAKES').
45: A device with a mode toggle caught ambiguously between two states, a big question mark above it (label 'WHICH MODE?'), and a surprised reaction. Tag: 'mind the mode'.
46: Two relay runners (agents) passing a baton; the baton is slipping in the gap between their hands, which is highlighted and labeled 'SEAM'. A note: 'failures live in the seams'.
47: A trust meter on the left rising step by step, driven by a track-record line graph climbing over time on the right (label 'EARNED'). A note: 'not granted'.
48: Side by side: a real open door (label 'ESCAPE HATCH', green check) and a fake door merely painted flat on a wall (label 'FABRICATED', red cross). A note: 'no exit means a made-up one'.
49: The same figure split by a dividing wall: on the left it wears a 'maker' hat (label 'AUTHOR'), on the right a 'grader' hat (label 'JUDGE'), kept separate. A red cross over an arrow that would let one be both.
50: A fact card on the right connected by an unbroken thread back to a labeled origin tag on the left (label 'SOURCE', green check). Beside it, a fact whose thread is cut, origin unknown (label 'LOST', red cross).
